source_paper_title: Time-Consistent Portfolio Selection for Rank-Dependent Utilities in a Constrained Market

Finally, it should be noted that investigating non-deterministic equilibrium strategies for portfolio selection under RDU is very challenging. We even do not know whether there is a non-deterministic equilibrium strategy.
title: Existence of non-deterministic strict equilibrium portfolio strategies under rank-dependent utility
background_latex:
Consider a finite-horizon continuous-time market on a filtered probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\{\mathcal F_t\}_{t\in[0,T]},\mathbb P)\) supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). There are \(n\le d\) risky assets and one risk-free asset. With constant coefficients \(r\in\mathbb R\), excess return vector \(\mu\in\mathbb R^n\), and volatility matrix \(\sigma\in\mathbb R^{n\times d}\) satisfying \(\sigma\sigma^\top\) positive definite, the wealth process under a (self-financing) portfolio-proportion strategy \(\pi=(\pi_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) evolves as
\[
\frac{dX_t}{X_t} = \bigl(r + \pi_t^\top\mu\bigr)\,dt + \pi_t^\top\sigma\,dW_t,\qquad X_t=x>0.
\]
A strategy \(\pi\) is an \(\mathbb R^n\)-valued progressively measurable process satisfying the usual square-integrability condition \(\int_0^T \|\pi_t^\top\sigma\|^2dt<\infty\) a.s. (and the corresponding integrability needed for the preference functional below).

Preferences are given by rank-dependent utility (RDU) over terminal wealth \(X_T\). Let \(U:(0,\infty)\to\mathbb R\) be CRRA utility,
\[
U(x)=\begin{cases}\frac{1}{\gamma}x^\gamma,&\gamma\ne 0,\\ \log x,&\gamma=0,\end{cases}
\]
and let \(w:[0,1]\to[0,1]\) (or time-dependent \(w(t,\cdot)\)) be a strictly increasing, continuously differentiable probability-weighting function with \(w(0)=0\), \(w(1)=1\) and mild growth control on \(w'\) ensuring finiteness of the RDU functional.

For a random variable \(X\), let \(Q_X:(0,1)\to\mathbb R\) denote its quantile function. At time \(t\), the agent evaluates terminal wealth by the conditional RDU functional
\[
J(t,x;\pi)=\mathbb E_t^{w}[U(X_T^{\pi})] := \int_0^1 U\bigl(Q^{t}_{X_T^{\pi}}(p)\bigr)\,dw(p)
\]
(with \(dw(p)\) replaced by \(w(t,dp)\) in the time-dependent case), where \(Q^{t}_{X_T^{\pi}}\) is the conditional quantile of \(X_T^{\pi}\) given \(\mathcal F_t\).

Time inconsistency is handled via a game-theoretic equilibrium notion. Given a candidate strategy \(\pi\), a deviation at time \(t\) over a short interval \([t,t+\varepsilon)\) is represented by \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}\) defined by \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}_s=\pi_s+\kappa\) for \(s\in[t,t+\varepsilon)\) and \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}_s=\pi_s\) otherwise, where \(\kappa\) is any bounded \(\mathcal F_t\)-measurable \(\mathbb R^n\)-valued random vector.

A strict equilibrium strategy (SES) is an admissible strategy \(\pi\) such that for every \(t\in[0,T)\) and every such \(\kappa\ne 0\),
\[
\lim_{\varepsilon\downarrow 0}\ \operatorname{ess\,sup}_{\varepsilon'\in(0,\varepsilon)}\ \frac{J(t,x;\pi^{t,\varepsilon',\kappa})-J(t,x;\pi)}{\varepsilon'}<0\quad\text{a.s. for all }x>0.
\]
In the paper, deterministic SES (DSES) are those SES with deterministic right-continuous \(\pi_t\), and they are characterized via (possibly singular) ODEs. The question below concerns existence of SES beyond the deterministic class.
problem_definition_latex: Determine whether there exists an admissible \(\mathbb R^n\)-valued progressively measurable strategy \(\pi\) that is a strict equilibrium strategy (SES) for the continuous-time portfolio problem with rank-dependent utility as defined above, and that is not (a.s.) equal to any deterministic function of time (i.e., \(\pi\) is genuinely non-deterministic). Equivalently, characterize existence (and, if possible, construction) of non-deterministic SES in this RDU portfolio-selection setting (in particular in incomplete markets \(n<d\), but also in general).
latex:
Consider a finite-horizon continuous-time market on a filtered probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\{\mathcal F_t\}_{t\in[0,T]},\mathbb P)\) supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). There are \(n\le d\) risky assets and one risk-free asset. \[

Time inconsistency is handled via a game-theoretic equilibrium notion. Given a candidate strategy \(\pi\), a deviation at time \(t\) over a short interval \([t,t+\varepsilon)\) is represented by \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}\) defined by \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}_s=\pi_s+\kappa\) for \(s\in[t,t+\varepsilon)\) and \(\pi^{t,\varepsilon,\kappa}_s=\pi_s\) otherwise, where \(\kappa\) is any bounded \(\mathcal F_t\)-measurable \(\mathbb R^n\)-valued random vector.

Determine whether there exists an admissible \(\mathbb R^n\)-valued progressively measurable strategy \(\pi\) that is a strict equilibrium strategy (SES) for the continuous-time portfolio problem with rank-dependent utility as defined above, and that is not (a.s.) equal to any deterministic function of time (i.e., \(\pi\) is genuinely non-deterministic). Equivalently, characterize existence (and, if possible, construction) of non-deterministic SES in this RDU portfolio-selection setting (in particular in incomplete markets \(n<d\), but also in general).
location_in_paper: Section 5 (Concluding Remarks), page 29 (PDF numbering as shown in the provided text).
open_problem_quote: Finally, it should be noted that investigating non-deterministic equilibrium strategies for portfolio selection under RDU is very challenging. We even do not know whether there is a non-deterministic equilibrium strategy.
subject_classification: Time-inconsistent stochastic control in mathematical finance
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The object is an equilibrium existence question over an infinite-dimensional space of progressively measurable controls, not a finite combinatorial structure. Disproving existence would require constructing a general obstruction rather than finding a small numerical instance.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: There is no clear computable fitness function for candidate stochastic strategies because verifying the strict equilibrium condition involves an essential supremum over all bounded local deviations and a small-time limit, which is analytically delicate and not directly checkable by simulation at scale.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
